source_jobs.title: Onboarding Guide (Patient Enrollment), Spanish/English Bilingual
source_jobs.description:
A doctor refers a family to Hera. They're cautiously optimistic but mostly overwhelmed - they've been bounced between systems, handed pamphlets, told to "call this number." They don't know what geriatric care management is. They definitely don't know it's covered by Medicare. They're one confusing phone call away from saying "never mind, we'll figure it out ourselves."

You're the reason they don't.

As an Onboarding Guide, you're the first voice a family hears from Hera. You help them understand how Hera competently cares for their parents, walk them through every step of getting started, and hand them off to their Hero feeling confident, heard, and ready. You're part closer, part concierge, part care coordinator. You just make the path so clear and the experience so easy that saying yes feels obvious.

This role sits at the intersection of relationship-building and operations. You're selling, but what you're selling is something families genuinely need, and your job is to remove every obstacle between a referral and a first appointment. Missing paperwork, skeptical adult children, a patient who's never used a computer - you will get through whatever it takes so that the family can meet their Hero.

Your mission as the Onboarding Guide

 - You're the reason families actually get help instead of falling through the cracks between referral and care

 - You aren’t just going through a motion. You're building trust with real families at a vulnerable moment

 - You own the full onboarding experience, no handing things off and hoping for the best

 - You leverage technology to make onboarding seamless, not a stack of faxes and voicemails

 - Elevate your ceiling. We're early stage and growing fast - Guides joining now are shaping how every future family experiences Hera

The Role

 - 1099 flexibility, 30–40 hrs/week in business hours EST (you set your schedule)

 - $25-30/hour base + enrollment bonuses (top performers earn $40-50/hour)

 - Must be fluent in both Spanish and English

 - Be the first point of contact after a doctor refers a family to Hera - guide them from "what is Hera?" to their first Hero appointment

 - Remote - must be located in the United States

What you'll actually do

 - Convert referrals into active clients by helping families understand what Hera is and how we can be valuable in their care journey

 - Guide families through the onboarding process - paperwork, documentation, insurance verification, scheduling - so nothing stalls

 - Coordinate between families and Hera's care team to ensure a clean handoff to their matched Hero

 - Help families navigate technology: uploading documents, joining telehealth appointments, signing e-forms

 - Spot friction in the intake process and fix it - not just for one family, but for every family after them

 - Collaborate with our engineering team to streamline workflows and scale how many families we can onboard without sacrificing quality

You'd be a great fit if you

 - Have a background in healthcare sales, care coordination, or customer success — especially with older adults or their families

 - Are warm on the phone and sharp on the follow-through. Families trust you quickly and nothing falls through the cracks

 - Think "how do I get this from 2 hours to 15 minutes" without cutting corners

 - Hold yourself to a high bar: done right, not just done

 - Are tech-savvy and energized by fast-moving environments where the playbook is still being written

We're on a mission to change the way we care for our parents and redefine a universal human experience. Watch our launch video here. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nUGD6cawQfk

source_jobs.title: Founding Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE GAP NO ONE HAS FILLED

Every complex system in America has a profession whose job it is to be your trusted guide. You have a lawyer for legal. An accountant for taxes. A financial advisor for your money. Healthcare, a trillion-dollar industry that's arguably the most complex and touches every single family, leaves us on our own.

99% of care happens when someone steps outside the four walls of a doctor's office. Hera is creating a new profession in healthcare to fill that gap: the Hero, a dedicated senior care expert for your family. Our Heroes educate, coordinate, and advocate for the most vulnerable population in the healthcare system. We’re covered by insurance, so we can serve every family in America.




BUILDING THE MOST TRUSTED INTERFACE OF HEALTHCARE

We are in the business of trust.

We're designing for the most emotionally charged moments of our customers' lives. The bar isn't just delight, it's trust. Hera's brand promise is simple: care worthy of our parents. This promise is our moat.

It's what physicians refer to, families buy, what Heroes deliver, and what the product must unlock and enforce at every touchpoint.




THE SUPPLY CHALLENGE

There aren't enough experts to serve every family who needs us. The bottleneck isn't demand, it's supply.

In order to scale to all of America, we need to unbundle expertise from the human and broaden the aperture of who can become a Hero. Anyone that can foster human connection should be able to become a Hero with Hera. In that world, our supply challenge becomes how we uphold our brand promise across every touchpoint with our customers.




OUR PRODUCT CHALLENGE

We believe the best software is invisible. We serve two audiences that most tech startups never build for: social workers and nurses as well as aging parents and their adult children. Trust is built or broken in every interaction, and the product is where that trust lives. We need to equip our Heroes to deliver expert-level care at every interaction.

Healthcare navigation runs on tribal knowledge: one social worker knows the trick to expediting Medicaid in New York, another knows which home visiting physical therapist families actually love. This expertise lives in people’s heads, fragmented across thousands of practitioners. We’re building the product that changes that.

Our platform captures every interaction, every solved problem, every hard-won insight from our expert care managers in the field — and turns it into compounding intelligence that makes every user on the platform smarter. Think Waymo’s approach to autonomy: high-quality human experts do the driving today, and every mile they log trains the system so that tomorrow, anyone — even someone with zero healthcare background — can perform like a 20-year veteran. The engineering challenge is building a platform deeply entwined and additive to the care management workflow with no lost nuance and perfect behavioral capture. From there we build a knowledge system that knows what to trust, what’s gone stale, what’s local vs. universal, and how to surface exactly the right insight at the right moment. Get it right, and you’ve built the platform that finally scales healthcare expertise beyond the lucky few who happen to know the right person.




OUR FOUNDING TEAM

We've raised 34M from Accel and Bain Capital Ventures and 10’xed revenue over the last 6 months organically through close partnerships with Weill Cornell, NYU Langone, UCSF, USC, and Mount Sinai. Chief of Geriatrics at Weill Cornell and Director of Geriatrics at NYU Langone have joined us as clinical advisors. CFO of Mount Sinai has invested in us.
The customer feedback is the most positive our team has seen in our careers. Along the way, we threw an art show for an 88 year old artist that went viral https://ny1.com/nyc/all-boroughs/news/2026/02/19/88-year-old-brooklyn-painter-sells-art-for-the-first-time and a talent show https://www.instagram.com/reel/DZi3YmESZ6Q/?igsh=MTR3cjg4ZmJvb2NyZQ%3D%3D wher

source_jobs.title: Telemedicine Physician, 50-State Licensed — Geriatric Care
source_jobs.description:
This is not a typical physician role.

If you've felt that traditional healthcare pressures clinicians to churn volume and don't actually shape patient outcomes, Hera is for you.



An older adult gets discharged from the hospital—you already know what happens. The family scrambles. Nobody talks to each other. Medications get confused. A fall happens. Another admission. The system wasn't designed for this moment. We started Hera because we felt it wasn't enough.



Hera is dedicated care management in the community for older adults, covered by Medicare. Every family gets matched with a dedicated Hero (care manager) who has the tools, time, and autonomy to deliver ongoing support. You're the clinical entry point—the comprehensive assessment that sets the clinical foundation for real, ongoing intervention.



The Role

We're building the clinical infrastructure that makes great geriatric care exceptional. As a Clinical MD, you'll conduct telehealth assessments that serve as the clinical gateway for families newly enrolled with Hera. Each 45-60 minute visit establishes the clinical foundation: confirming Medicare eligibility, synthesizing complex medical and social history, identifying barriers to treatment, and flagging clinical priorities for our care managers (Heroes) to address over months of ongoing coordination. This assessment work directly shapes the care strategy that families receive.



Key Responsibilities

• Conduct comprehensive telehealth assessments for newly enrolled Medicare clients

• Synthesize complex medical histories and identify clinical priorities for ongoing care coordination

• Document clinical findings that inform care manager (Hero) intervention strategies

• Provide clinical oversight and collaboration as needed with care management team

• Establish clinical credibility and trust during initial patient contact



Why clinicians tell us Hera feels different

• Clinical autonomy. You own your assessments. No templates, no utilization pressure, no inbox drowning. You conduct real clinical work that shapes care plans.

• Remote work from home. Set your own flexible schedule. No clinic overhead, no commute, no admin burden.

• You're building it. You're not an employee in a machine. You're a founding clinical leader shaping how we do geriatric care in new states.

• You're never alone on a case. A whole care team (Heroes, social workers, coordinators) executes the plan you establish.

• No ceiling on you. We're early stage and growing fast. Clinical leaders joining now are shaping the company.



Requirements

• MD or DO (must be licensed to practice in all 50 states)

• Board certification in family medicine, internal medicine or other relevant speciality.

• Substantial experience in geriatric medicine, primary care, or chronic disease management

• Confident in telehealth assessment and skilled at building clinical rapport across a screen

• Strong clinical judgment and comfort with complexity and ambiguity

• Aligned with our mission: you see older adults as deserving of excellent, attentive care



Ready to shape geriatric care delivery at scale? Apply now.

source_jobs.title: Head of Clinical
source_jobs.description:
THE GAP NO ONE HAS FILLED

Every complex system in America has a profession whose job it is to be your trusted guide. A lawyer for legal. An accountant for taxes. A financial advisor for your money. Healthcare, a trillion-dollar industry that touches every single family, leaves us on our own.

99% of care happens when someone steps outside the four walls of a doctor's office. Hera is creating a new profession in healthcare to fill that last mile gap: the Hero, a dedicated care manager for your family. Our Heroes educate, coordinate, and advocate for the most vulnerable population. We're covered by insurance, so we can serve every family in America.




QUALITY IS THE STRATEGY

We serve the most complex, highest-acuity population in healthcare: older adults coming straight out of a hospital discharge, living with dementia, juggling a dozen medications and twice as many specialists. When families meet us, they are facing the hardest experience of their entire lives.

Our brand promise is simple: care worthy of our parents. That promise is our moat. It's why physicians refer, why families join, and what every Hero must deliver on every visit.

We are creating a new category. Winning is how we make sure the standard of care for this generation of parents is one worthy of them.

That is why this role exists. The Head of Clinical at Hera is the person who defines what great care means at Hera, and makes sure we deliver it for every family.




OUR FOUNDING TEAM

We've raised $34M from Accel and Bain Capital Ventures and 10x'ed revenue over the last 6 months organically through close partnerships with Weill Cornell, NYU Langone, UCSF, USC, and Mount Sinai. The Chief of Geriatrics at Weill Cornell and Director of Geriatrics at NYU Langone have joined us as clinical advisors. CFO of Mount Sinai has invested in us.

You'll build Hera alongside co-founders Jenny, Myles & Connie and a team from Palantir and Headway. We are in person in NYC. Read our story here https://hellohera.com/about.




THE ROLE

You are the clinical leader of Hera. From day one, the full care team — Heroes and their clinical supervisors — report to you, and clinicians practice under your medical direction. You own the standard of what worthy care looks like, how it's measured, and how it gets delivered. And you'll stay close to the work: you'll see patients occasionally, because the person setting the standard of care should never be far from the work.



Three charters to start with the opportunity to grow into Hera’s CMO:

 1. Own the care model and run the care team. You define how Hera keeps clients safely at home and out of the hospital — what great care means for this population, how it's delivered consistently across every Hero, and how clinical oversight continues long past the first visit. You set the standard, you build the systems that measure it in partnership with engineering and ops, and when the bar isn't met, you're the one who acts.

 2. Be the clinical brain of the product. Most clinical leaders write training modules and scale through rigid processes that allow things to slip through the cracks. You'll do something different: partner with our engineering team to encode clinical judgment directly into our AI platform so that quality scales through software, not headcount. This is the part of the job that has never existed before, and it's the reason the right person will find this the most interesting clinical role in the country.

 3. Define and shape a new category. We meet most of our families through health systems. You make Hera the most credible clinical voice in the room with health system leaders and payers, and you turn our care model into the industry's reference point. Long-term, you help position Hera as the category creator in longitudinal care management: the missing piece of American healthcare.





WHO YOU ARE

 - You're a physician. MD required, board certified in internal medicine, geriatrics, or family medicine. You'll serve as

source_jobs.title: Founding GTM (evangelist)
source_jobs.description:
THE GAP NO ONE HAS FILLED

Every complex system in America has a profession whose job it is to be your trusted guide. A lawyer for legal. An accountant for taxes. A financial advisor for your money. Healthcare, a trillion-dollar industry that touches every single family, leaves us on our own.

99% of care happens when someone steps outside the four walls of a doctor's office. Hera is creating a new profession in healthcare to fill that last mile gap: the Hero, a dedicated senior care expert for your family. Our Heroes educate, coordinate, and advocate for the most vulnerable population. We’re covered by insurance, so we can serve every family in America.

 


BUILDING HEALTHCARE'S MOST TRUSTED INTERFACE

We are in the business of trust.

We're designing for the most emotionally charged moments of our customers' lives. Hera's brand promise is simple: care worthy of our parents. This promise is our moat. It's what physicians refer to, families buy, what Heroes deliver, and what the product must unlock and enforce at every touchpoint.

 


OUR FOUNDING TEAM

We've raised 34M from Accel and Bain Capital Ventures and 10’xed revenue over the last 6 months organically through close partnerships with Weill Cornell, NYU Langone, UCSF, USC, and Mount Sinai. Chief of Geriatrics at Weill Cornell and Director of Geriatrics at NYU Langone have joined us as clinical advisors. CFO of Mount Sinai has invested in us.
The customer feedback is the most positive our team has seen in our careers. Along the way, we threw an art show for an 88 year old artist that went viral https://ny1.com/nyc/all-boroughs/news/2026/02/19/88-year-old-brooklyn-painter-sells-art-for-the-first-time and a talent show https://www.instagram.com/reel/DZi3YmESZ6Q/?igsh=MTR3cjg4ZmJvb2NyZQ%3D%3D where every act is over 65.
You’ll build Hera alongside co-founders Jenny https://www.linkedin.com/in/jennydlee/, Myles https://www.linkedin.com/in/mylesnovick/ & Connie https://www.linkedin.com/in/jingyi-connie-kang/ and a team from Palantir and Headway. We are in person in NYC. Read our story and watch our launch video here https://hellohera.com/about.



THE ROLE

We're calling this role Founding Evangelist for a reason. This isn't GTM or sales in the conventional sense. We're convincing geriatricians, social workers, nurses, population health leaders, and health system executives to entrust us with their most vulnerable patients. The only way that works is if the person across the table believes, in their bones, that we will care for those patients better than anyone else. Evangelism is the right word because the work is half conviction, half craft.

 - From day 1, you own the health system referral engine. This is an IC-first role. That means mapping out the ecosystem of partners: who refers, who champions, and what each persona cares about. You'll build and run outreach to new health systems in New York, New Jersey, and in our national expansion.

 - You'll build the playbook ... and invent what hasn't been done. Some of what works will look like classic BD: stakeholder mapping, in-services, executive relationships. But some of the most powerful things we've done don't look like sales at all. This summer we're throwing a talent show called Encore where every performer is over 75, a community event that's also a partnership wedge. We want someone who can think from first principles and find creative ways to stand out and scale. The best ideas in this role probably aren't in any sales playbook you've read.

 


WHO YOU ARE

 - Partners trust you fast. You genuinely give a damn about this mission and people can feel that. Partners refer to us because they believe we'll take care of their patients; that belief lives in you first.

 - You are absolutely relentless, but never transactional. You play the long game. You'll engineer an introduction over weeks or find the most unconventional way in. You treat "no" as information, not an answer. But every move is in service of building tru

source_jobs.title: Founding Growth Marketer
source_jobs.description:
THE GAP NO ONE HAS FILLED

Every complex system in America has a profession whose job it is to be your trusted guide. You have a lawyer for legal. An accountant for taxes. A financial advisor for your money. Healthcare, a trillion-dollar industry that's arguably the most complex and touches every single family, leaves us on our own.

99% of care happens when someone steps outside the four walls of a doctor's office. Hera is creating a new profession in healthcare to fill that gap: the Hero, a dedicated senior care expert for your family. Our Heroes educate, coordinate, and advocate for the most vulnerable population in the healthcare system. We’re covered by insurance, so we can serve every family in America.




BUILDING THE MOST TRUSTED INTERFACE OF HEALTHCARE

We are in the business of trust.

We're designing for the most emotionally charged moments of our customers' lives; the bar isn't just delight, it's trust. Hera's brand promise is simple: care worthy of our parents. This promise is our moat.

It's what physicians refer to, families buy, what Heroes deliver, and what the product must unlock and enforce at every touchpoint.




HOW WE GROW TODAY

Today Hera primarily grows through the most trusted referral in healthcare: a physician or social worker. We're embedded at NYU Langone, Weill Cornell, and Mount Sinai. Departments reach out unprompted. We show up in health system newsletters we didn't pitch.

That's a powerful foundation, and a ceiling. The families who need Hera most aren't only the ones a doctor sends us. Growth marketing is how we reach the rest of them: adult children in the middle of a caregiving crisis, searching at midnight. Families who don't know they can get help like this, covered by Medicare.

This role is how Hera gets to them.




EARLY BRAND WINS

 - Viral community moments: we organized an art show for an 88-year-old man, Ben Feldshuh, and the TikTok https://www.tiktok.com/@hellotohera/photo/7603858166669004063 went viral. NY1 https://ny1.com/nyc/all-boroughs/news/2026/02/19/88-year-old-brooklyn-painter-sells-art-for-the-first-time & NBC covered it; on the night of the show, every single one of Ben's 40+ paintings sold and over 300 people came. We’ve now turned this into an ongoing series, Hera Bridges.

 - Released our “Age Your Way” launch video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nUGD6cawQfk, featuring 5 fabulous New Yorkers - Elizabeth, Andrea, Gladys, Stanley, and Michael aging with joie de vivre. The video was highlighted by Director’s Library https://directorslibrary.com/2026/latest/campaigns/age-your-way-hera/.

 - $0 spent on customer acquisition with 95% customer retention




WHAT THIS ROLE IS

Great growth marketing is rare because it requires two things that don't often live in the same person: excellent creative instinct and analytical rigor. In this hire, we're looking for both.

You'll own the full B2C growth engine: strategy, budget, creative, measurement – working alongside existing storytelling and brand efforts to make sure everything we put into the world is true to who we are.




WHAT YOU'LL DO: BUILD THE GROWTH ENGINE

 - Create content that earns attention: emotive, informative, and highly shareable.

 - Own SEO and paid acquisition across social and search, from creative to bid strategy to post-mortem.

 - Run rigorous experiments and treat every campaign as a hypothesis.

 - Design attribution frameworks that give us a true picture of what's working.

 - Build the systems that let one person do what used to take a team: automated workflows, AI-assisted production, feedback loops that compound.

 - Manage budget with precision and the confidence to scale what's working fast.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have exceptional taste and a curatorial eye, and also run towards the data.

 - You’re a generalist by disposition, and can flex from high-level strategy to the nitty-gritty.

 - You’re great at editing – knowing exactly what to strip away to make something land.

 - You're a fi

We are in person in NYC. Read our story and watch our launch video here https://hellohera.com/about.







JOIN OUR APOLLO UNIT TO DO HARD THINGS WITH US

We are looking for an ambitious and relentless generalist problem solver to join us on this mission. At other companies this role is often called Business Operations and Strategy. We call it Apollo Unit, drawing inspiration from the mythological figure, to represent the team ethos - surgical execution, relentless truth seeking, and obsession with greatness. You will go where Hera needs you the most, often into unknown terrains, and figure things out. Build the Hero growth engine. Launch new marke

source_jobs.title: Hera Hero
source_jobs.description:
This is not a typical social work or nursing role. If you've felt that traditional healthcare roles have a ceiling on you, and you want to actually bridge the gaps for older adults after they leave the office or facility, Hera is for you.

An older adult gets discharged from the hospital and you already know they'll be back in 30 days. A family leaves overwhelmed, clutching a stack of papers with numbers they'll never get through. You did everything you could within the system and it still wasn't enough. We started Hera because we felt the system was letting down the generation that raised us.

Hera is dedicated care management in the community for older adults, covered by Medicare. Every family gets matched with a dedicated Hero, you, who actually has the tools, time, and autonomy to help. It’s not a 15 min monthly check-in call. It’s real, ongoing support where you see things through and families call you their Hero.

Why Heroes tell us this feels different

 - Autonomy to manage your own client load: you own your families to give them the care they deserve instead of hoping for the best

 - Remote work from home, set your own flexible schedule

 - Delightful technology built for you so you can focus on fostering connection with families, not fighting an EMR

 - You're never alone on a case with supportive training and community of other Heroes

 - No ceiling on you. We're early stage and growing fast, Heroes joining now are shaping the company.

The Role

 - 1099, 30–40 hrs/week in business hours (you set your schedule)

 - $30–60 / hour: factors include experience, geography, and client load

 - Serve older adults and their families in aging at home safely

You'd be a great fit if you are

 - Hold an LMSW, LCSW, or RN (not required but a plus)

 - Have experience in geriatric care management, hospice, palliative care, discharge planning, rehab, or hospital social work

 - Love fostering connection with older adults and their families

 - Are a creative problem solver who wants a job that looks different every day

 - Are tech-savvy and excited by quickly evolving environments and leveraging AI to supercharge your work

 - Remote - must be located in the United States


We’re on a mission to change the way we care for our parents and redefine a universal human experience.
Watch our launch video here. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nUGD6cawQfk

notes: Hera is a healthcare startup with a mix of technical and non-technical roles, including engineering, growth marketing, and clinical positions.
